channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta is against California’s AI bill. Here’s why
item.description: California lawmakers are considering legislation that would require artificial intelligence companies to test their systems and add safety measures so they can’t be potentially manipulated to wipe out the state’s electric grid or help build chemical weapons — scenarios that experts say could be poss
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Can our government avoid a ‘Don’t Look Up’ scenario?
item.description: NASA recently held its annual exercise to game out next steps if an asteroid or other astronomical body is on a threatening course with Earth—a mission seen as the agency’s top priority by 60% of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz, Fast Company

item.title: A24's Financial Strength Reflects Its Artistic Edge in Indie Film
item.description: As one of the most respected film studios, A24 has excelled at producing critically acclaimed, low-budget movies with artistic integrity, thanks to its strategic financial backing and strong partnerships with auteur filmmakers.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Crazy Domains adds GenAI to its website builder
item.description: Building a website with Crazy Domains has just gotten crazy simple with the help of generative artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Frustrations are being aired about Windows 11’s new Copilot app – but here’s why we’re not worried (just yet)
item.description: Is Copilot going backwards? Accusations of a ‘downgraded’ AI are being flung around, but what’s really going on?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New strategy, A-123 update to help reduce improper payments
item.description: David Lebryk, the fiscal assistant secretary at Treasury, said a new strategy provides tools, best practices and guidance to improve federal payments. The post New strategy, A-123 update to help reduce improper payments first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Zero Trust Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2024
item.description: Where are agencies in their zero trust journey? The post Federal Executive Forum Zero Trust Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh adult industry's challenge to Texas law
item.description: The Supreme Court has indicated it will take up a case next term involving the adult entertainment industry’s challenge to a Texas law requiring pornography sites to implement age-verification measures.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Anthropic launches fund to measure capabilities of AI models
item.description: AI research is hurtling forward, but our ability to assess its capabilities and potential risks appears to be lagging behind.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Biden administration provides $504 million to support 12 technology hubs nationwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Tuesday that it was providing $504 million in implementation grants for a dozen technology hubs in Ohio, Montana, Nevada and Florida, among other locations.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 10 ways AI can make IT more productive
item.description: When it comes to maximizing productivity, IT leaders can turn to an array of motivators, including regular breaks, free snacks and beverages, workspace upgrades, mini contests, and so on.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: UN weather agency says Tropical Cyclone Freddy that hit eastern Africa last year was longest ever
item.description: The U.N. weather agency says it has confirmed that Tropical Cyclone Freddy, a deadly Indian Ocean storm that lashed eastern Africa last year, was the longest-lasting cyclone ever recorded — at 36 days.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 AI-Related Job Concerns Facing Employees: Strategies for Mitigating Anxiety
item.description: As AI implementation accelerates in the workplace, employees are grappling with concerns about job security, control, and future prospects. This article addresses common fears surrounding AI adoption and provides practical advice on how to adapt and thrive in an AI-driven environment.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: French antitrust charges threaten Nvidia amid AI chip market surge
item.description: Nvidia is at risk of facing charges from the French antitrust authority for alleged anti-competitive behavior, a potential first in regulatory actions against the AI chip giant, according to a report by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Lenovo adds new AI solutions, expands Neptune cooling range to enable heat reuse
item.description: With the growth of artificial intelligence in data centers of all types comes not only requirements for applications and services, but the need to cool the power-hungry systems that run those workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How evolving AI regulations impact cybersecurity
item.description: While their business and tech colleagues are busy experimenting and developing new applications, cybersecurity leaders are looking for ways to anticipate and counter new, AI-driven threats. It’s always been clear that AI impacts cybersecurity, but it’s a two-way street.
item.dc:creator: Ram Movva and Aviral Verma, Info World

item.title: Hijos de Rivera aúna supercomputación e IA para investigar “los sabores del futuro”
item.description: Hijos de Rivera está desarrollando un ambicioso proyecto de investigación en el que, según relatan, por primera vez a nivel mundial se ha utilizado la computación cuántica y la inteligencia artificial (IA) para realizar una simulación de la percepción del sabor.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: UN adopts Chinese resolution with US support on closing the gap in access to artificial intelligence
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations General Assembly adopted a Chinese-sponsored resolution with U.S.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California considers unique safety regulations for AI companies, but faces tech firm opposition
item.description: California lawmakers are weighing a bill that would regulate powerful artificial intelligence systems.
item.dc:creator: Trân Nguyễn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Security Breach Hits Patelco Credit Union, Locks Out Half Million Members
item.description: A security breach at Patelco Credit Union has left approximately 500,000 members unable to access their online accounts, affecting their ability to check balances, send/receive money and change passwords.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Martin, The Mercury News

item.title: OpenSSH vulnerability regreSSHion puts millions of servers at risk
item.description: Researchers have uncovered a serious remote code execution vulnerability in the Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH) server that could let unauthenticated attackers obtain a root shell on servers and take them over.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: How customer experience can transform the Defense Department
item.description: Integration of all data elements about each user, combined with integrated systems and processes are all required for digital transformation. The post How customer experience can transform the Defense Department first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Eclipse Theia IDE arrives for desktop, browser
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has announced the release of the Eclipse Theia IDE. Based on the Theia cloud and desktop IDE framework implemented in TypeScript, the Theia IDE is avialable for Windows, Linux, and macOS and can be tried online.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta's 'Pay or Consent' Ad Model Found to Violate EU Tech Rules, Preliminary Findings Show
item.description: The European Union has determined that Meta's 'pay or consent' advertising model for European users contravenes the Digital Markets Act, according to preliminary findings released by the bloc.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The technology powering Taylor Swift, Netflix and the Sphere
item.description: Disguise is a pioneer in an exploding market for visual graphics in demand from Hollywood to the NBA.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The need for Supreme Court review grew more dire when federal appeals courts reached opposite conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday wiped lower court rulings regarding two state laws aimed at barring social media companies from banning users based on their political views, kicking the major First Amendment challenge down the road.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: European Union accuses Facebook owner Meta of breaking digital rules with paid ad-free option
item.description: European Union regulators have accused social media company Meta Platforms of breaching the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apple's Artificial Intelligence Business: A Lucrative Opportunity?
item.description: As AI revolutionizes industries, Apple is poised to capitalize on its potential by building a business around it. Will this strategic move pay off or prove costly in the long run?
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Instagram creators could soon make AI versions of themselves
item.description: Think you've seen them? Meta said “AI” will appear in front of the creator’s name followed by a “beta” tag after.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Too many passwords to remember? Here's what you can do
item.description: According to some unscientific studies, the average person has hundreds of passwords. That's a lot to keep track of.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Supreme Court nears the end of another momentous term. A decision on Trump's immunity looms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the last 10 days of June, on a frenetic pace of its own making, the Supreme Court touched a wide swath of American society in a torrent of decisions on abortion, guns, the environment, health, the opioid crisis, securities fraud and homelessness.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As AI gains a workplace foothold, states are trying to make sure workers don't get left behind
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — With many jobs expected to eventually rely on generative artificial intelligence, states are trying to help workers beef up their tech skills before they become outdated and get outfoxed by machines that are becoming increasingly smarter.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Amazon is reviewing whether Perplexity AI improperly scraped online content
item.description: Amazon is reviewing claims that the artificial intelligence startup Perplexity AI is scraping content — including from prominent news sites — without approval.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court Sides with Biden Administration in Social Media Dispute Against Conservative States
item.description: The US Supreme Court rules in favor of the Biden administration, limiting the federal government's ability to regulate social media content on COVID-19 and election security topics, in a dispute with Republican-led states.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A Vatican Radio territory in north Rome once blamed for electro-smog will become a solar farm
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis decreed Wednesday that an area of northern Rome, long the source of controversy because of electromagnetic waves emitted by Vatican Radio towers there, will now house a field of solar panels to fuel Vatican City. Citing the Vatican’s pledge in U.N.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI image-generator startup Stability AI looks for reshoot with new CEO and investor group
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The troubled artificial intelligence company behind image-generator Stable Diffusion is looking for a reshoot with a new CEO and a surge of investment from a group that includes Sean Parker, Facebook’s former president and Napster's co-founder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
